summary: Polish active Node/CycleTLS wrapper read-only answers for recent orders, ranked Thai stores, and cheapest chicken parmesan menu search.
reason: bricenice17’s first real read-only test exposed that order items were not first-class, store rankings lacked enriched rating/delivery fields, and cheapest menu item answers required ad-hoc probing instead of a direct command.
validated_outcome: 2026-05-14T15:53Z: npm run build passed using /home/hermes/.local/bin/npm; generated Go skeleton checks passed with /home/hermes/.local/bin/go test -vet=off ./... plus go build for both CLI and MCP commands; active installed wrapper doctor/help passed with authenticated=true, csrf_present=true, consumer_id_present=true; orders recent --limit 2 --json returned itemDetails; stores rank Thai --limit 5 --json returned enriched ranked stores with normalized rating displays; items search "chicken parmesan" --store-query "italian" --stores 5 --limit 10 --json returned dinner_or_pasta and sub_or_sandwich cheapest buckets. No cart/checkout/order mutation commands were run.
